content:
Implementation & Engineering Quality
Code structure, error handling, reproducibility, and security hygiene in the Gateway snapshot.
7.00 / 10.00
20%	
Code is clean and well-organized with clear module boundaries (src/client.ts, src/config.ts, src/errors.ts, src/tools/*). Error handling is comprehensive via MmxcError class with proper MCP code mapping. Tests use undici MockAgent for offline verification. Strict TypeScript with noUncheckedIndexedAccess. No plaintext secrets detected. The CyOps activity (1 execution run, 127M+ tokens) roughly aligns with the medium-complexity codebase. The 1-run count is on the lower side for this scope but the plan document in docs/plans/ and the comprehensive test suite (8 test files) support a credible build story.

Show less
Architecture & Complexity Fit
Module boundaries, data flow, and design proportionate to scope — from source tree and docs.
8.00 / 10.00
16%	
Clear layering: transport (src/index.ts) → server factory (src/server.ts) → tool registry (src/tools/index.ts) → individual tools (src/tools/*.ts) → shared HTTP client (src/client.ts) → config (src/config.ts) → error normalization (src/errors.ts). The architecture clearly serves the MCP bridge product goal. The bonus-tools feature flag is cleanly separated. Async video with polling lifecycle is well-decomposed. Cross-field validation in music is properly separated from schema. The agent.md contributor standards align with the implementation.

Show less
Deliverable Completeness
README feature checklist vs actual files, code, and artifacts present in the snapshot.
6.00 / 10.00
20%	
README claims music generation as ✅ supported, but src/tools/music.ts:135-140 explicitly states 'music generation is declared and validates input, but the live API call has not been wired up in this build.' This is a README/repo mismatch for the music feature. Other core tools (image, speech, video) are fully implemented with mocked lifecycle tests. The 15+ host installation blocks and feature table are comprehensive. The plan document in docs/plans/ accurately describes scope. Deducting for the honest-but-undisclosed gap in music generation.

Show less
Project Copy & Documentation
Clarity of README/docs text — problem, solution, usage, structure (repo text only).
8.00 / 10.00
16%	
README.md (16,931 chars) provides clear problem→solution→how-to-run flow. Features table, authentication precedence, CLI options, installation blocks for 15+ MCP hosts (Open WebUI, Claude Desktop, Cursor, Codex, Gemini, etc.), and per-tool parameter tables. agent.md provides contributor standards. The plan document in docs/plans/ is detailed with 13 acceptance criteria and a verification matrix. A newcomer can onboard from text alone.

Show less
AI/Agent Integration (Repo Evidence)
Agent workflows in repo plus CyOps platform token usage (cross-check with repo evidence).
8.00 / 10.00
20%	
The product IS the AI agent integration — MMXOmni is an MCP server that gives AI agents 'superpowers' in media creation. Confirmed CyOps token usage: 127,329,007 total tokens across 60 events (claude-minimax-m3: 115.5M in/382K out, codex-minimax-m3: 11.3M in/72K out). agent.md documents agent standards. The plan document in docs/plans/ references 'any coding agent' workflows. High token count and substantive agent config/docs in repo tell a coherent story. MCP is the substrate for AI agent tool use, and the product is a tool bridge.

Show less
Implementation Innovation
Novel technical approach visible in code, architecture, or documented design choices.
6.00 / 10.00
8%	
Several visible technical differentiators: (1) async-first video with optional wait=true polling, mcp_code=5 timeout convention; (2) bonus-tools feature flag with env+CLI precedence; (3) mmx-cli-compatible error code mapping (1/3/4/5/10); (4) per-tool embed=true for MCP-native base64 vs URL asset return; (5) cross-field validation in music (at-least-one of prompt/lyrics, instrumental+lyrics mutex). These are well-thought-out but represent solid engineering rather than a strongly novel approach — the core pattern is a standard MCP server wrapping a REST API.

Show less

Improve our score
did you git commit and push already?
